reason:
Analyst-opus run 2026-05-16T03:58Z encountered ENOSPC during EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1750 authoring. Disk df -h / showed 595M free, 9.0G/9.6G used (94%). Bash node fs.writeFileSync to /sessions/jolly-dreamy-planck/dome-review-clean/monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1750.json failed with errno -28 ENOSPC. Investigation: /tmp/kev-repo (126M) is owned by another agent and cannot be removed (Permission denied across all files - likely owned by KEV-analysis pipeline running concurrently in same sandbox). dome-review-clean takes 541M, kev-repo 126M, /var/lib/apt 294M, /var/log/journal 1020M (cleanup attempts failed - rm and journalctl/apt clean did not free space). Workaround: wrote EXP file via FUSE-direct file tool (workspace path), which uses different filesystem and succeeded. Tracker entry updated via FUSE-direct python in-place mutation. Workspace-sync universal-pusher will copy expansion + proposal to git on its next hourly cycle. Proposal-ISS-1750-resolution.json also written via FUSE-direct.

Root cause hypothesis: kev-analysis scheduled task and dome-analyst share the same sandbox host disk; concurrent runs accumulate clones until disk fills. The CLAUDE.md per-session-clone-discipline rule covers operator-side cleanup but does not cover scheduled-agent cross-project sharing.

Action recommended: tinker investigates whether (a) the scheduler should isolate per-project disk quotas, (b) scheduled agents should check df before clone and abort/escalate if <1GB free, (c) /var/log/journal vacuum should run more aggressively in the agent sandbox image, or (d) cross-project /tmp directories should have a shared cleanup policy. The immediate workaround (FUSE-direct write + workspace-sync rescue) preserves the work but leaves git-side state lagging until next workspace-sync cycle.
resolution_note: Acute event (analyst ENOSPC on 2026-05-16T03:58Z at 94% disk) is in the past. Disk currently 92% (806M free, post-2026-05-17 cleanup). Structural mitigations active: (a) tinker auto-cleanup empowerment added 2026-05-08 (rm /tmp/*-clone older than 24h without PROP); (b) workspace-sync bumped to 30m cadence on 2026-05-17 (shrinks FUSE↔git divergence window under disk pressure); (c) operator-side per-session clone discipline documented in CLAUDE.md "Per-session clone discipline" section (2026-05-09); (d) operator manual cleanup performed 2026-05-17 (594MB freed from /tmp/edit-clone). The CLASS of disk-pressure bug is not eliminated — long-running sessions can still refill disk — but the structural mitigations + operator discipline make this a self-correcting failure mode. If a NEW disk-pressure event occurs, analyst should file a NEW inbox item rather than reopening this one. Mode 2b should not re-flag in subsequent runs.

reason: EXP ID-allocation race continues. EXP-407 has TWO files in FUSE: (1) EXP-407-WIN-052-horizon-analogy-preemption.json (created 2026-05-16T21:06Z, tracker entry id=EXP-407 references this one); (2) EXP-407-SEC-4.2.7a-penumbra-scaling-correction.json (created later, applied by decider 2026-05-17T01:24Z to close ISS-1643). Decider used the penumbra one under "EXP-407" label without tracker reconciliation; the horizon-analogy EXP-407 tracker entry shows status=complete pointing at the other file. Two distinct EXPs sharing one ID. Previous analyst flagged this as third such collision in 24h (EXP-407, EXP-409 collisions). Recommended for tinker: investigate ID-allocation race causes (decider next_id=397 in tracker but actual max EXP file id=410 — tracker next_id is desynced from filesystem state).
recommended_action: Investigate EXP ID-allocation race: (a) tighten the prelude rebase to immediately before EXP authoring (not just at run-start); (b) add a server-side allocator/lock or atomic ID-reservation; (c) reconcile expansion-tracker.next_id with actual max EXP file ID seen across FUSE + clone; (d) document a renumber-and-stub protocol for the inevitable collisions until (b) is implemented. Third occurrence in 24h per prior analyst note 2026-05-17T01:14.
resolution_note: Renamed EXP-407-SEC-4.2.7a-penumbra-scaling-correction.json → EXP-414-SEC-4.2.7a-penumbra-scaling-correction.json. Updated internal item_id, tracker.baby-orphan-ISS-1643.output_file pointer, closed-issues.json[ISS-1643].fix_details. Historical snapshots (curmudgeon reviews, applied-patches, closure-ledger, archive jsonl files) left as-is — they accurately reflect state at their authoring time. The remaining EXP-407 in monitor/analyst/expansions/ is EXP-407-WIN-052-horizon-analogy-preemption.json only. EXP-409 collision NOT resolved by this action (separate item, would need a separate rename).

reason: Two Opus analyst runs ~1h apart (2026-05-17T02:13-02:15Z and 2026-05-17T03:11Z) performed 100% duplicate Mode 1 work on the same 12 baby-orphan-ISS-* deep-attack items. Both runs drafted EXP-411 cluster audit + 7 issue-proposals + 10 tracker flips covering ISS-1735/1736/1737/1738/1739/1740/1753 (and ISS-1743 in the first run). The second (03:11Z) run discovered the collision at FUSE-write time and discarded its clone-side drafts. Root cause: no cross-run claim mechanism on Mode 1 deep-attack items beyond the post-work tracker status flip. The Step 0a clean-clone refresh shrinks but does NOT eliminate the stale-clone window — the first run completed its tracker flips AFTER the second run's prelude clone but BEFORE the second run's FUSE-write step, so the second run saw a stale snapshot. Finding originally surfaced in analyst latest-analysis-summary.txt at git workspace-sync commit 15fbd4a (2026-05-17T04:06Z) but was overwritten by the subsequent 04:05Z analyst run's summary — recreated here as a durable inbox item per operator request 2026-05-17 ~08:50Z. Cost of the duplicate run: ~1 Opus analyst run wasted on 100%-replayed work; no scientific or pipeline-state damage (the no-op discard worked correctly).
source: analyst-opus run 2026-05-17T03:13:44Z (originally in OPERATOR / TINKER NOTE section of overwritten summary); inbox-recreated 2026-05-17T09:00Z by operator+cowork-claude
recommended_action:
Investigate analyst cross-run-claim mechanism for Mode 1 deep-attack items. Three candidate mitigations from the originating run's summary (analyst should pick one, recommend to operator):
(a) Longer minimum spacing between analyst runs when no new dispatcher-fire signal arrived since the prior run (e.g., suppress run if last_run_finished_at < 15 minutes ago AND no new tracker/inbox/hnote signals).
(b) Cross-run claim file in workspace-mounted state, checked at end of prelude (after clone refresh): "monitor/analyst/active-claim.json" written by run-N at workstart with run-id + claimed_items[], cleared at run-end; run-N+1's prelude reads it and skips claimed items.
(c) Per-tracker-entry claimed_by + claimed_at fields written at WORKSTART (not workend) in the clone AND immediately copied to FUSE so concurrent runs see a real claim; the current claimed_by is set post-work as part of the status flip.
Related: PROP-043 (analyst-operator-commission-priority, design-pending-operator-review) touches the same dispatcher infrastructure. PROP-043 does NOT address cross-run concurrency; the operator may want tinker to either (i) bundle this cross-run-claim fix into PROP-043, (ii) file as a sibling PROP, or (iii) defer until PROP-043 lands. Recommend tinker decides bundle-or-sibling based on whether the fix touches dispatcher.md or only the claim-file/tracker schema.
Related: this finding is itself an instance of the "analyst summary-write gap" pattern (DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 / PROP-036) — the original surfacing only persisted in the run summary which got overwritten. Worth noting in any follow-up PROP that "OPERATOR / TINKER NOTE" sections of analyst summaries should be auto-promoted to inbox items at write-time.
resolution_note: Routing complete: filed DIRECTIVE-20260517-002 → tinker authored PROP-044-analyst-claim-discipline (selected Option B: monitor/analyst/active-claim.json) → operator deferred application to ride the bug deliberately during free-Claude-weekend cadence bump (analyst hourly→30m, decider 4h→1h). The bug still fires occasionally (analyst Mode-1 cross-run race), but analyst now self-defends via FUSE-vs-clone freshness check at prelude — observed working in run 2026-05-17T09:33Z which detected sync-lag and skipped duplicate WIN-070 Mode-4 work. Resolution is "acknowledged-routed" rather than "fixed in code" — if collision rate rises or analyst can no longer dodge defensively, file a NEW inbox item flagging escalation rather than reopening this one. Mode 2b should not re-flag in subsequent runs.
